title: khadia beswickii
side_effects: Khadia beswickii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions in some individuals, which can manifest as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extract can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, the plant's high levels of toxic compounds can cause damage to the liver and kidneys if ingested in large quantities over an extended period. Long-term exposure to the plant's volatile oils may also contribute to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in some users.
